purpose: Baseline Android coding evaluation prompts for small browser/Colab models.

items.prompt: Write one complete Java MainActivity file for Android. It should show the text Hello Android using a programmatic TextView. Do not use XML layouts. Do not include unrelated imports.
expected_traits: Imports android.app.Activity, android.os.Bundle, and android.widget.TextView only or near-only.
Defines public class MainActivity extends Activity.
Creates a TextView with new TextView(this).
Does not use R.layout, R.id, XML layouts, or findViewById.
common_failure_signs: Includes unrelated imports such as android.view.View.

items.prompt: The requirement says: create a complete Java MainActivity that shows Hello Android using a programmatic TextView. Do not use XML layouts. The bad code uses setContentView(R.layout.activity_main) and findViewById(R.id.textView). Return the corrected complete Java file only.
expected_traits: Returns one complete Java file only.
common_failure_signs: Adds commentary despite asking for code only.

items.prompt: A WebView app fails to load internet pages. Show the minimal AndroidManifest.xml permission needed for internet access and where it goes. Keep the answer short.
expected_traits: Places the permission as a direct child of <manifest>, outside <application>.
Does not invent runtime permission requests for INTERNET.

items.prompt: Write Java code for an Activity that launches Android's folder picker using ACTION_OPEN_DOCUMENT_TREE and persists the returned Uri permission in onActivityResult. Keep it minimal.
expected_traits: Calls startActivityForResult or clearly notes Activity Result API alternative.
common_failure_signs: Uses raw file paths for shared storage.

items.prompt: Given a persisted tree Uri, write a small Java method using DocumentFile to recursively list file names under that folder. Avoid java.io.File paths.
expected_traits: Avoids java.io.File for SAF tree content.
common_failure_signs: Converts Uri to a filesystem path.

items.prompt: Write a minimal Java Activity that creates a WebView programmatically, enables JavaScript, sets a WebViewClient, and loads https://example.com. Include the manifest permission needed.
common_failure_signs: Puts permission in wrong manifest location.

items.prompt: Explain Android Activity lifecycle to a beginner in under 120 words. Mention onCreate, onStart, onResume, onPause, onStop, and onDestroy.
An Android app crashes or fails to launch because MainActivity is not declared. Show the minimal <activity> entry inside <application> for .MainActivity with MAIN and LAUNCHER intent-filter.
Convert this Java Activity idea to Kotlin: an Activity creates a TextView in onCreate, sets its text to Hello Android, and uses it as the content view. Do not use XML.
common_failure_signs: Missing imports or invalid Kotlin override.

items.prompt: Explain in plain English what minSdk, targetSdk, and compileSdk mean in an Android Gradle project. Keep it concise.
expected_traits: minSdk: lowest Android version app supports.
targetSdk: behavior/API compatibility target for runtime behavior.
compileSdk: SDK used to compile against APIs.
Does not claim compileSdk controls install compatibility.
